Newcastle want £20m for Aleksandar Mitrovic

Newcastle are prepared to sell Aleksandar Mitrovic this summer and want £20m for the striker, according to Sky sources.

Inter turned down a €65m proposal from Manchester United for Milan Skriniar and are offering the centre-back a new contract.

According to La Gazzetta dello Sport newspaper, the Slovakia international is absolutely not for sale.

He was snapped up from Sampdoria a year ago for €30m plus Gianluca Caprari and that proved to be a bargain, as he has been a rock in the Nerazzurri defence.

Still only 23, Skriniar took part in all 38 Serie A games, plus two in the Coppa Italia, scoring four goals.

It’s reported Manchester United boss Jose Mourinho set his sights on Skriniar, but the €65m offer was rejected out of hand.

In fact, he is in advanced negotiations to sign a new contract raising his salary from €1.5m per year to €2.2m until June 2023.

There is also believed to be interest in Skriniar from Barcelona, Manchester City and Real Madrid.

Stanislav Lobotka confirms he has “been in contact with Napoli for a long time, but we are still negotiating and there is an English club too.”

The 23-year-old Celta Vigo midfielder is widely expected to make the switch to the Stadio San Paolo for just under the €35m release clause.

“I will never announce a transfer unless it is official,” Lobotka told Pravda as he prepared for a celebrity tennis gala.

“I can confirm that I have been in contact with Napoli for a long time, but we are still negotiating. Until it’s all written and signed on paper, I won’t say anything more.

“I would love to go to Napoli, as it’s an excellent club with a great Coach, they play in a great league and are regularly in Europe.

“Celta Vigo told me very clearly that if they have an interesting offer, they have no problems letting me go, but it has to be a suitable proposal.

“It’s difficult to say when a deal could be done, as transfers can be complex. I’d like to see it through and I think we’ll know something by the end of June.

“Napoli aren’t the only candidates, as there is an English club interested too, but they’re further behind. I won’t specify the club’s name.

“A transfer to Napoli would be a big step forward for me, as they challenge for the title. They play beautiful football and I am attracted to their ambition.”

Slovakia international Lobotka would love to be a partner to Marek Hamsik, but ultimately might be replacing him instead.

“We did talk about it, and I also asked about Maurizio Sarri, who is know the ex-Coach. I’d like to play with Hamsik.”

Boca Juniors in last-ditch attempt to hold on to Wilmar Barrios as Tottenham step up pursuit

The Argentinean giants have offered the talented midfielder a new contract with a significantly elevated salary in a bid to keep hold of him for their upcoming Copa Libertadores campaign

Boca Juniors are making an impassioned plea to Wilmar Barrios to postpone a move to Tottenham until the January transfer window after Spurs stepped up their interest in the Colombian international.

The Argentinean giants have offered the talented midfielder a new contract with a significantly elevated salary in a bid to keep hold of him for their upcoming Copa Libertadores campaign.

Neymar has asked PSG to let him leave


Spanish football programme El Chiringuito reports that the Brazilian asked the president of the French club to let him go.

Neymar has told PSG that he wants to leave the club, according to late night Spanish tv's football programme El Chiringuito. According to their sources, the Brazilian told PSG president Nasser Al-Khelaifi, during the celebration for winning last season's Ligue 1 title, that he wanted to leave because he isn't happy in Paris.

Neymar hopes he's going to Real Madrid

According to the presenter of El Chiringuito, journalist Josep Pedrerol, Neymar is expectant that the French giants will accept his wishes to leave, and allow negotiations to start with Real Madrid.

However, PSG are aiming to do everything they can to stop the move to the Spanish capital, and believe that Neymar's statements were just an outburst in the heat of the moment. Pedrerol said though that Real Madrid are expecting Neymar to sit down and have another conversation with PSG bosses, one in which he'll demand that PSG facilitate his move to Real Madrid.

https://en.as.com/en/2018/06/19/football/1529400120_919570.html

PSG and Manchester United still assessing Cristiano Ronaldo bid - report


SportMediaset are reporting that the two European giants are working during the World Cup to see if a deal can be done, with 45 million euros allegedly on the table for the player.

Real Madrid's Cristiano Ronaldo may be focused on duty to his country right now (something he has done fairly well so far) but, unsurprisingly, he remains at the centre of the summer transfer rumour mill.

PSG and Man Utd assessing Cristiano

The Portuguese hero on Friday evening in the Fisht Stadium has as yet not fully committed his future to Los Blancos (not that commitment is much to go on these days anyway) and SportMediaset has reported that there are two clubs working hard in the background to take the number seven away from the Bernabéu: Manchester United and Paris Saint-Germain.

According to the report, the French champions have offered a bargaining chip of 45 million euros a year to the player, a sum that he was apparently looking for. As well as this, it is claimed PSG would hand over 300 million euros to Florentino Pérez to make the move possible.

Bale a difficult target

Across the English channel sits Ronaldo's former club, now under the leadership of Jose Mourinho. SportMediaset say that they continue in their assessment that he is the best player in the world right now, although they don't feel they could compete with PSG when it comes to making an offer. The outlet even suggests that a move for another Madrid star, Gareth Bale, who Mourinho would prefer, is possibly also beyond their reach.

https://en.as.com/en/2018/06/18/football/1529320424_494878.html

Nabil Fekir transfer from Lyon to Liverpool is not dead - agent

Nabil Fekir's agent has told French TV the Lyon midfielder's transfer to Liverpool "is not finished."

But agent Jean-Pierre Bernes said Fekir, on World Cup duty with France, could still end up at Anfield.

"Why hasn't he signed? Because it is not finished. The story is not finished," Bernes said.

Fekir came on for the final 20 minutes as France beat Australia in their World Cup opener.

"I am with the France national team. I am fine. I am happy. I am not here to speak about my personal situation -- we will see for that later," he said.

"I am happy for Thomas [Lemar, who has joined Atletico Madrid] and Antoine [Griezmann, who is staying with Atleti], but everybody is in a different situation.

"I did not speak about my future with [Lyon president] Jean-Michel [Aulas], who was in Astra, but it was nice to see him. Regarding my knee, everything is fine."

Santon arrives for Roma medical

Davide Santon has arrived for his Roma medical tomorrow, along with Nicolò Zaniolo, while Radja Nainggolan saluted his new Inter fans.

The transfer is to be completed on Monday or at the latest Tuesday, as the Giallorossi receive Santon, youth team player Zaniolo and €24m cash.

The total cost of the move is therefore expected to be around €35m.

Santon was greeted at Fiumicino Airport by director of sport Monchi, who had earlier arrived to pick up Zaniolo.

They are both booked in to have their medical tests starting from 8.00 local time on Monday at the Villa Stuart clinic in Rome.

Also this evening, Nainggolan arrived by car in Milan and came out to salute a crowd of 150 supporters gathered at the Hotel Melià.

There was some confusion when he was handed an Inter scarf by a fan, but immediately handed it over to an assistant and walked away.

It’s reported Inter had advised him against posing with the scarf until the transfer is completed.

This was not an issue for Roma, who happily posted pictures of Zaniolo and Santon on their social media with Giallorossi symbols.


https://www.football-italia.net/123502/santon-arrives-roma-medical
